rationale_en: Annual re-issuance of the standing import/export bylaw for one trade year is a procedural continuation, not a rate change, so no expected effect on customs duty revenue.
Deleting one phrase from a narrow military-vehicle exemption clause is a negligible administrative tweak with no expected effect on customs revenue.
A single 10-billion-rial credit line to a labor bank is a small, one-off transaction, negligible against the aggregate government debt to the Central Bank.
Amending one note of the agricultural credit act is a real but narrow adjustment to farm credit supply, a marginal force among many shaping agricultural output and rural development.
This decree only sets the technical procedure for partitioning state shares of jointly-owned estates ahead of transfer, an administrative implementing detail with no direct macro channel to GDP.

It is a genuine operational piece of the land-redistribution program, but as a narrow partition procedure it has only a partial, indirect expected effect on agricultural output and land-wealth statistics.
Adding four clauses to the standing import/export bylaw is a moderate but still routine regulatory tweak, with no expected visible effect on customs duty revenue.
Two added tariff notes to one article of a free-zone bylaw is a narrow, procedural customs adjustment with no expected effect on import duty revenue.
A note amendment to a war-migrant employment credit bylaw is a small, targeted administrative tweak with no expected effect on aggregate labor statistics.
This bylaw only governs repayment mechanics of an existing bond scheme, an administrative detail with no expected effect on capital market aggregates.
A routine incremental amendment to the standing import/export bylaw, procedural in nature with no expected effect on customs duty revenue.
Revising one article of the import/export bylaw is a narrow tariff-classification tweak with no expected visible effect on customs duty revenue.
Another routine administrative revision to the standing import/export bylaw, with no expected effect on customs duty revenue.
Revising one procedural article of the import/export bylaw, negligible and administrative, with no expected effect on customs duty revenue.
A licensing/procedural detail change to one article of the import/export bylaw, negligible against aggregate trade statistics.
A minor adjustment to a free-zone duty collection mechanism, procedural and negligible against aggregate customs duty revenue.
An implementation-procedure tweak to free-zone duty collection instructions, negligible against aggregate customs duty revenue.
Converting a clause into a standalone article within the import/export bylaw is a structural reorganization of the text, not the substance, with no expected effect on customs revenue.
A procedural update to the decree implementing the import/export bylaw, administrative and negligible against customs duty revenue.
Adding one note to a free-zone duty-collection clause is a minor administrative adjustment with no expected effect on customs duty revenue.
A routine update to the standing import/export bylaw, administrative in character with no expected effect on aggregate trade statistics.

This bylaw operationalized Iran's largest privatization program, a national-scale structural reform of enterprise ownership that we would expect to leave a material, visible mark on GDP and investment.
Article 44 privatization used stock-exchange listing as its principal transfer mechanism, so this bylaw is a major, direct instrument acting on listed-company count and market capitalization.
The bylaw's effect on private business formation runs through a diffuse channel, reduced state footprint, that is real but only one of many forces shaping the investment climate.
This directive expanded a specific bond-trading channel on the exchange, a real but partial contribution to overall capital-market activity.
Formalizing commodity trading on the Mercantile Exchange added a real new market segment, one force among several shaping capital-market aggregates.
Authorizing a single new bond instrument (ijarah) is a marginal addition to the capital market, too small a channel to expect a visible effect.
This directive regulates the marketing chain for live livestock but does not set prices, so no expected direct effect on the recorded livestock price series.
Settling one tranche of agency foreign-currency debt has a real but partial effect on external debt stock and FX demand, one administrative action among many driving these series.
A 1944 wartime rationing bylaw is historical context for scarcity-driven price gaps, not a direct instrument acting on the modern exchange-rate series.
This wartime rationing bylaw predates the modern CPI series and is background context rather than a direct price-control instrument on it.
An administrative court ruling on off-network fuel purchases is a narrow enforcement action with no expected visible effect on aggregate subsidy spending.
A judicial interpretation of one value-added-duty question is a narrow legal clarification with no expected effect on aggregate tax revenue.
Setting the import duty rate for one product category (vehicles) is a real but partial force within the broader customs-revenue and weighted tariff aggregates.
Raising vehicle import duties improves the relative price position of domestic automakers, a real but partial protective channel among several shaping motor-vehicle production.

This amendment restructured the post-revolutionary state banking system's relationship to the Central Bank, a real but partial force on the government-debt-to-CBI balance next to other post-revolution financing decisions.
As part of the foundational restructuring of the post-revolutionary financial sector, this amendment is a major instrument we would expect to leave a material mark on monetary and credit aggregates.
Reshaping credit allocation across the whole banking system has a real macro channel to GDP, but it is one force among the many revolutionary-era disruptions of that period.
A technical fiscal-year extension for nationalized banks is a procedural bookkeeping matter with no expected effect on government debt to the Central Bank.
This fiscal-year extension is an administrative technicality within the nationalized-bank framework, with no expected effect on monetary aggregates.
A single 3-billion-rial loan to one bank is a small, discrete transaction, negligible against aggregate domestic credit or government debt to the Central Bank.
This clause directly writes the indexation rule, requiring the guaranteed price to rise at least with inflation, that defines how this exact wheat procurement price series is set going forward.
The guaranteed-purchase-price regime covers many basic crops, so this amendment has a real but more diffuse effect spread across the broader agricultural producer-price basket.
Extending administrative deadlines under an existing land-reform bylaw is a procedural continuation with no expected visible effect on agricultural land statistics.
Raising the loan ceiling for tobacco and tea growers is a real financing channel for these two crops, but one of several forces shaping their output.

Converting Bank Sepah into a joint-stock company reshaped one of the named deposit banks' commercial lending capacity in exactly the years this chart compares, a real but partial effect within the aggregate deposit-bank comparison.
Creating a dedicated foreign-trade bank is a real institutional addition to the banking system, but a single new institution is only a partial force on monetary aggregates.
A trade-finance bank facilitating transactions is a modest, indirect channel with no expected visible effect on aggregate trade growth rates.
A one-year retroactive export bonus payment is a small, targeted fiscal incentive with no expected visible effect on aggregate export trade.
This bylaw governs how effectively assessed customs duties and taxes are actually collected, a real but partial administrative factor within total customs revenue.
A barter-import preference clause changes the composition, not the level, of duties collected, with no expected visible effect on import statistics.
This law governs how one institution, the Housing Foundation, administers and transfers housing units, a real but partial contributor to the decades-long census count of housing stock.
This financing enabled the Isfahan steel mill's capacity, a major, direct instrument behind the large step-change visible in Iran's crude steel production series.
Ratifying an expanded operating area for the oil consortium is a real but partial contribution to production and rents alongside the many other contract and field decisions of the era.
A 50% ownership transfer within one offshore concession consortium is a company-level change with no expected effect on national oil production volume.
This law is itself a concrete allocation decision of the exact oil-revenue-by-recipient mechanism this chart tracks, a major direct instance of the measured series.
Financing the IGAT-II pipeline is a real infrastructure investment with a plausible future channel to gas rents, but it is one of several forces shaping the gas sector's development timeline.
Statutory water tariffs for one utility sector are a small administered-price component, negligible against the aggregate consumer price index.
Amending the national medium-term development plan touches investment and spending targets across the whole economy, a real but partial force among the many drivers of GDP in this period.
Sectoral industrial targets and privatization timelines in the plan amendment have a real but indirect channel into industrial and manufacturing value added.
Setting the internal staffing and administrative rules of one ministry is an institutional-capacity matter, too indirect to expect a visible effect on agricultural output.
Amending one clause of a single year's budget law is a real but small piece of that year's statutory budget, marginal against the recorded annual budget totals.
This is another narrow clause-level amendment to one year's budget law, a marginal contribution to that year's recorded budget totals.
A parliamentary rejection preserves the status quo rather than changing it, so there is no expected effect on agricultural output or land statistics.
As a rejection of proposed changes, this document records that nothing changed in the land-reform regime, so no expected effect on GDP is implied.
This directive sets a technical pricing methodology for fund-held securities, a reporting/valuation rule with no expected effect on capital-market aggregates.
Tightening one enforcement article against currency smuggling has a real but narrow channel to the parallel-market exchange-rate gap, one of many enforcement measures.
Anti-smuggling enforcement can shift some flows into recorded trade statistics, but the effect is small and indirect for aggregate merchandise trade.
This is an amendment to the executive bylaw implementing one clause of a single year's budget law, an administrative implementing detail with no expected effect on budget totals.
Amending the executive bylaw for one budget-law note is an implementing detail, negligible against the recorded annual budget totals.
This amends the bylaw for a named earmarked-revenue mechanism, a real but narrow channel into government revenue and tax collection.
Adding two notes to one clause of a budget execution bylaw is an implementing detail, negligible against the recorded annual budget totals.
Licensing rules for brokerage firms shape the intermediary base of the stock market, a real but partial structural contribution to capital-market activity.
An addendum to the implementing bylaws of two budget-law articles is a procedural detail, negligible against the recorded annual budget totals.
Approving futures contracts as a tradable instrument adds a new product line to the exchange, a real but partial contribution to overall capital-market turnover.
Adding one note to a budget execution bylaw clause is an implementing detail, negligible against the recorded annual budget totals.
This punitive decree against currency and goods smuggling has a real but narrow deterrent channel to the parallel exchange-rate gap and recorded trade flows.
A violations-handling procedure for one securities-law article is a compliance detail, negligible against capital-market aggregates.
Rules on the use of offering-advisory services are a narrow regulatory detail, negligible against overall capital-market activity.
Amending the free-trade-zone founding decree touches the incentive structure meant to attract FDI and trade through the zones, a real but partial force among broader FDI and trade drivers.
Increasing the paid-in capital of one specialized agricultural bank is a narrow, indirect widening of one credit channel, negligible against aggregate private-sector credit.
This amends the core legal instrument for domestic public borrowing, a real named channel for the state's debt financing, though a partial force alongside other financing decisions of the period.
A minimum-tariff arrangement for one bilateral trading partner is a narrow, historical technicality with no expected effect on aggregate import statistics.
Setting customs ground-rent and warehousing fees is a real cost component of clearing imports, a partial contributor to the customs-clearance efficiency measure.

A direct customs-duty exemption for vegetable oil imports is a named channel lowering the landed cost of exactly this commodity, a real but partial effect alongside global prices and other trade-policy factors.
A criminal-punishment statute aimed at protecting farm activity during upheaval is a deterrent measure, not an economic instrument, with no expected visible effect on production indices.
A single year's national budget law is one of many routine annual instruments, a small force against the economy's overall output even in this early period.

Explicitly withdrawing the meat-price subsidy in Tehran and Isfahan is a direct administered-price change for exactly this commodity, though a real but partial effect since it covers two cities rather than the whole national market.
This is a narrow civil-service staffing law for one technical school's graduates, with no plausible economic channel to national GDP.
A staffing law for one technical school's graduates has at most a tangential, unquantifiable connection to rural development, with no expected effect on the measure.
Completing certain articles of the land reform act is real land-policy activity in the immediate post-revolution period, but one of many disruptive forces behind the 1980 GDP contraction.
This amendment directly governs land-tenure and redistribution terms, a real but partial channel into agricultural land-use statistics.

As part of the opening legislative act of the Shah's land-reform program, this belongs in the GDP narrative for the era, but like land reform generally its precise macro channel is diffuse and hard to expect as a visible move in the line.
Amending Article 10 directly changes land-redistribution terms, a real but partial channel into farm-size and land-tenure statistics.
This bylaw sets the mechanism for securing and collecting customs duties, a real but partial administrative factor within total customs revenue and applied tariff rates.
Removing one cost component (asphalt duty) on domestically assembled cars is a real but partial contributor to the cost environment of Iran's growing auto industry.
Forgiving rangeland fee claims is a modest, one-off debt-relief measure for herders, negligible against aggregate livestock output or prices.
Tightening anti-money-laundering compliance is a narrow regulatory adjustment, negligible against aggregate broad money.
This bylaw only governs banking operations within the free trade zones, a narrow subset of the economy, negligible against national credit or trade aggregates.
This is the implementing bylaw for one article of a broader credit-restructuring law aimed at industrial firms, a real but partial channel into private-sector credit and sectoral value added.
